file_insights.purpose: Protocol definition and abstraction layer for AI providers

key_components.name: AIService
key_components.responsibility: Route generation requests to registered providers

file_insights.purpose: Anthropic Claude implementation of AIProvider protocol

key_components.name: ClaudeProvider
key_components.responsibility: Implement AIProvider interface for Claude API
features: Error handling for RateLimitError and APIError

patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables multiple provider implementations without coupling to specific types
Flexible provider selection at runtime based on model_key
patterns_discovered.extensibility: New providers can be registered without code changes

patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures proper resource cleanup and supports streaming semantics
patterns_discovered.safety: Automatic cleanup via context manager protocol

patterns_discovered.benefit: Distinguishes rate limit errors from general API errors with appropriate logging
patterns_discovered.logging: Warnings for rate limits, errors for other API failures

patterns_discovered.location: DEFAULT_MODEL constant + constructor override + env var support
patterns_discovered.hierarchy: Constructor param > env var > hardcoded default

patterns_discovered.compatibility: Works with async for loops for natural iteration

gotchas_discovered.impact: Errors on initialization rather than first call, could fail fast in integration tests
gotchas_discovered.note: This is actually a best practice but could surprise users

gotchas_discovered.issue: Stream context manager must be properly closed
gotchas_discovered.impact: If iteration stops early, context manager handles cleanup automatically
gotchas_discovered.note: Async context manager handles this well, but incomplete iteration should be understood

gotchas_discovered.issue: Model selection flexibility could be confusing
gotchas_discovered.impact: Multiple ways to set model (constructor, env var, default) with different precedence

gotchas_discovered.issue: No retry logic for rate limits
gotchas_discovered.impact: RateLimitError is re-raised immediately without backoff
gotchas_discovered.note: Caller must implement retry strategy, no built-in exponential backoff

gotchas_discovered.issue: Provider registry doesn't validate protocol compliance
gotchas_discovered.impact: Non-protocol-compliant objects could be registered and fail at runtime
gotchas_discovered.note: Runtime checkable mitigates this but could add validation in get_provider

approach_outcome.description: Create AIProvider Protocol interface and AIService abstraction class with ClaudeProvider implementation
requirements_met: AIProvider Protocol interface created with runtime_checkable
AIService abstraction class with provider routing
Error handling for APIError and RateLimitError
Logging for errors and rate limits

recommendations.action: Add unit tests for AIService and ClaudeProvider
recommendations.rationale: Protocol-based design needs test coverage for protocol compliance
suggested_tests: Test error handling for missing models

recommendations.action: Implement exponential backoff retry logic for RateLimitError
recommendations.rationale: Rate limiting is common in API usage; automatic retry improves resilience
recommendations.implementation_hint: Consider using tenacity or custom retry decorator

recommendations.action: Document the model selection precedence clearly
recommendations.rationale: Multiple configuration methods could confuse users about which takes priority
recommendations.specifics: Constructor param > env var > DEFAULT_MODEL constant

recommendations.action: Add provider factory/builder for cleaner instantiation
recommendations.rationale: Currently providers must be instantiated externally and registered

recommendations.action: Add runtime protocol compliance check in AIService.get_provider()
recommendations.rationale: Provides early detection of misconfigured providers
recommendations.implementation: Use isinstance(provider, AIProvider) check with helpful error message

recommendations.action: Add docstrings with usage examples for extending providers
recommendations.rationale: Makes it easier for future developers to add new AI providers
recommendations.includes: Step-by-step guide for implementing new AIProvider

recommendations.action: Consider adding metrics/tracing for provider calls
recommendations.rationale: Production usage will benefit from observability
recommendations.metrics: Token usage, latency, error rates per provider
